name: Slide:ology / Presentation Zen
summary: Content-driven, one idea per frame, full-bleed images, minimal text

color_palette.description: Presentation Zen does not prescribe a specific color palette. It advocates for restrained color use driven by content and imagery. Typical implementations use:
color_palette.accent: One accent color derived from brand or topic - for Vietnamese national unity, red (#B91C1C) or gold (#D97706) would be natural choices
color_palette.philosophy: Let the imagery set the color mood. Avoid decorative color that competes with photographs. Maximum 2-3 colors beyond black/white.
color_palette.note: The style is defined more by what it removes (gradients, multi-color schemes, decorative borders) than what it adds

recommended_families: Any clean sans-serif - Be Vietnam Pro ideal for Vietnamese content
Garr Reynolds uses Myriad Pro in his books, but advocates for whatever is most readable
typography.size_scale: Extremely large. Presentation Zen advocates for text large enough to read from the back of the room. Typical: headlines 72-120pt equivalent, body text 36-48pt equivalent. At 1920x1080: headline 80-120px, supporting text 40-56px.
typography.weights: Bold for headlines, Regular for minimal body text. Weight contrast replaces color for hierarchy.
typography.vietnamese_diacritics: No specific guidance - style is font-agnostic. Be Vietnam Pro works well with the large sizes advocated.

animation_style.approach: Minimal to none. Presentation Zen philosophy treats animation as distraction unless it serves the narrative. Preferred transitions: simple cut or gentle fade.
animation_style.entrance_patterns: Fade-in for text overlays on images. No bouncing, sliding, or spinning. Content appears, it does not perform.
animation_style.motion_budget: Very low. A 13.5-minute video in this style might have 5-10 total animated moments, each a simple opacity transition.
animation_style.note: This is the most animation-conservative style among all candidates

projector_readability.contrast_ratio: Typically excellent. Large white text on dark photography or dark text on white achieves 10:1+ ratios. Full-bleed images need careful selection to ensure text overlay areas have sufficient contrast.
projector_readability.minimum_font_sizes: This style naturally uses very large fonts (40px+ minimum), far exceeding projector readability requirements.
projector_readability.color_washout_resistance: Photography-dependent. High-quality, high-contrast images resist washout well. Low-contrast or pastel images suffer significantly on projectors.
projector_readability.risk: Text overlaid on busy images can become unreadable on washed-out projectors. Needs semi-transparent overlay behind text.

wcag_contrast_score.typical_combinations: White (#FFFFFF) on dark overlay (#000000 at 60-70% opacity): 8-12:1 depending on underlying image. Dark text (#1A1A1A) on white (#FFFFFF): 17.6:1.
wcag_contrast_score.rating: AA to AAA when executed properly, but highly dependent on image selection
wcag_contrast_score.degradation_note: With projector degradation, text on semi-transparent overlays may drop below AA if overlay opacity is too low. Recommend 70%+ opacity overlays.

projector_degradation_resistance.strengths: Large text sizes provide inherent readability margin. High-contrast images (e.g., dark cityscape, bold Vietnamese flag) remain effective.
projector_degradation_resistance.weaknesses: Subtle photographic details lost on projectors. Low-contrast artistic images become muddy. Text-on-image legibility degrades significantly if overlay contrast is insufficient.
projector_degradation_resistance.recommendation: Pre-test each slide's readability at reduced contrast. Use generous text overlays. Prefer high-contrast, bold imagery.

vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.quality: Excellent at the sizes this style uses. Vietnamese diacritics are never a problem at 40px+ font sizes.
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.line_height_needs: Large sizes mean generous line-height is natural. No clipping risk.
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.minimum_safe_size: This style's minimum (40px+) far exceeds the 28px floor needed for Vietnamese diacritics on projectors

cultural_fit.analysis: Full-bleed imagery of Vietnamese landscapes, people, cultural events, and national symbols creates immediate emotional connection to the national unity topic. One-idea-per-frame approach suits Vietnamese rhetorical style which values clear, principled statements. Risk: if images are generic stock photos, the cultural connection is lost entirely.
cultural_fit.adaptation_needed: Requires curating high-quality Vietnamese-specific imagery. Generic stock photos would undermine cultural authenticity.

cultural_semiotics.achievable_symbols: Full-bleed images of: Vietnamese flag (red + gold star), ethnic minority groups in traditional dress, national landmarks (Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, One Pillar Pagoda), rice paddies representing rural Vietnam, modern Ho Chi Minh City skyline representing development
cultural_semiotics.strength: Photography is the most direct way to invoke specific cultural symbols without abstraction
cultural_semiotics.risk: Image selection bias - easy to over-represent Kinh majority culture when topic is national unity across 54 ethnic groups

teacher_perception_signal.signal: Signals storytelling ability and visual sophistication. A well-executed Presentation Zen video says 'we understand how to communicate ideas clearly and memorably.' The minimal text approach demonstrates confidence in the material.
teacher_perception_signal.risk: If poorly executed (low-quality images, too little content), could signal laziness rather than restraint. Teacher may perceive insufficient theoretical depth if text is too sparse.

rubric_alignment.format_15_percent: Good - clean, professional format that demonstrates design awareness. Full-bleed images create visual impact.
rubric_alignment.theory_35_percent: Weak - this is the critical weakness. One-idea-per-frame with minimal text makes it difficult to present dense theoretical content. Socialist theory requires definitions, frameworks, citations - hard to fit in this style without modification.
rubric_alignment.application_50_percent: Moderate - application examples can be shown through photography and case study images, but data visualization (statistics, charts) conflicts with the full-bleed image approach.

content_density_capacity.words_per_frame: Very low: 10-30 words per frame by design philosophy. This is a feature, not a bug, for spoken presentations but problematic for a video where narration may be limited.
content_density_capacity.theory_support: Poor for dense theoretical content. Would need to break theory into many more frames than other styles, increasing video complexity.
content_density_capacity.data_viz_support: Weak - charts and data tables conflict with the full-bleed image philosophy. Would need dedicated data frames that break the visual pattern.

production_effort.migration_from_current: High. Current codebase is built around structured card-based layouts with DS components. Presentation Zen requires a fundamentally different approach: full-bleed images, minimal text overlays, fewer but larger visual elements.
production_effort.estimated_effort: 4-5 days. Requires sourcing 30-50 high-quality Vietnamese images, implementing full-bleed image components, text overlay system, and redesigning all section layouts.
production_effort.image_sourcing: Major effort - need rights-cleared, high-resolution Vietnamese photography. Stock photo costs or time to find CC-licensed alternatives.

remotion_implementation_complexity.css_needs: Low - mostly full-screen images with text overlays. CSS is simple (object-fit: cover, absolute positioning, text shadows).
remotion_implementation_complexity.three_js: Not needed. 3D background would be replaced by photography.
remotion_implementation_complexity.render_performance: Moderate concern - full-bleed high-resolution images increase memory usage during Remotion render. Need to optimize image sizes.
remotion_implementation_complexity.computation_cost: Low runtime computation. Main cost is image decoding.

distraction_level: Very low. The entire philosophy is about removing distractions. Content and imagery are the only visual elements.
differentiation: Moderate. While the philosophy is well-known, most Vietnamese student presentations use bullet-heavy PowerPoint. A properly executed Presentation Zen video would stand out significantly in a Vietnamese university classroom.

temporal_coherence.analysis: Visual coherence depends on consistent image quality and color grading across all photographs. Without a unifying color scheme or structural design language, 9 different speakers' sections risk feeling disconnected. Needs strong editorial direction on image style and color temperature.

dark_vs_light_mode_viability: Inherently flexible - images can be dark or light. For projector use, prefer images with sufficient contrast for text overlays. No fixed dark/light mode - each frame adapts to its image.

examples: TED talks frequently use Presentation Zen principles in their slide design
Apple keynote presentations follow similar minimal-text, large-image philosophy
Garr Reynolds' own presentations and books demonstrate the style extensively
Many academic conference keynotes (not student presentations) use this approach
precedent.academic_context: More common in professional/keynote contexts than student presentations. Unusual for Vietnamese university assignments, which adds differentiation but also risk of appearing unconventional.
